sections.paragraph:
ἠχώ, οῦς, ἡ (s. prec. three entries; Trag. et al.; Philostrat., Vi. Soph. 2, 3, 1; Herm. Wr. 444, 19 Sc.; Job 4:13; Wsd 17:18) sound, fig. πάλιν ἔσομαι ἠχ. I shall again be nothing but a sound IRo 2:1 (but ἠχώ in this pass. is only a conjecture by Bunsen, Zahn et al.). On Lk 21:25 s. ἦχος and ἠχέω.—DELG s.v. ἠχή.

prec. prec. = preceding

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 441
